page: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 4 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 12.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 260 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the processor runs at 2.0 GHz and features a 16 MB L3 cache, alongside hardware support for AVX, AES cryptography, and SHA_NI instructions.

Benchmark results indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core CPU performance is average. However, memory subsystem performance is a notable bottleneck; large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth are weak, and PassMark memory and database operations scores fall into the bottom 10%. Additionally, single-threaded and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speeds are poor.

In terms of workload execution, the server achieves an average text generation speed of 87.51 tokens per second for small 135M LLM models, though prompt processing remains weak. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput are also on the lower end. Given the shared CPU allocation and memory bandwidth limitations, this configuration is best suited for general-purpose workloads, light web serving, or development environments that benefit from NVMe storage but do not require sustained high-throughput memory access.
description: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd is an x86_64 virtual server from the High Performance family, configured with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 12.0 GB of RAM, and 260 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The processor operates at 2.0 GHz with a 16 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AES instructions. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, ranking in the top 25%, while multi-core performance remains average. Conversely, the server exhibits weak memory bandwidth on large blocks and poor performance in database operations and bzip3 decompression. This resource profile offers a cost-efficient entry point for general-purpose applications, development environments, and lightweight web serving that require fast local storage but can tolerate shared CPU allocation and constrained memory throughput.
og_description: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 12.0 GB of RAM, and 260 GB of NVMe SSD storage. It features strong single-core CPU performance and average multi-core capacity.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-amd is a virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 12.0 GB of RAM, and 260 GB of NVMe storage, suited for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An AMD EPYC virtual server combining strong single-core performance and NVMe storage for general-purpose and budget-conscious workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 12.0 GB of system memory
260 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Average text generation speeds for small-scale LLM inference
Suited for general-purpose applications and development environments
